title: oxytropis leucantha var. viscida
intro: Oxytropis leucantha var. viscida, also known as the glandless or glandless white locoweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the legume family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Oxytropis leucantha var. viscida include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and soothe skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional.
